_doc: Embedded tactical visuals for The 500+ Whop Stores Index. {palette} resolves at runtime.

embedded-1.notes: Part 4 lead visual. The 20-Niche Heatmap. Bubble or treemap with niche names sized by store count.
embedded-1.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference visualization showing The 20-Niche Heatmap of all 512 Whop stores. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. No font names, no typography labels, no instructions inside the image.
3. Spell every word and number exactly as written.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 20-Niche Heatmap"
SUBTITLE: "512 Whop stores cataloged. Bubble size = store count."

COMPOSITION: a treemap-style packed-bubble layout filling the lower 75 percent of the frame. 20 rounded-rectangle bubbles, each labeled with the niche name and store count, sized proportionally to the store count. Bubbles tile together with small consistent gaps to read as a coherent treemap, not floating circles.

The 20 niches and their store counts (use these exact strings inside each bubble: niche name above, store count below):

1. "Trading" / "59" (the largest bubble, top-left of the treemap)
2. "Self-Improvement" / "54" (second largest, adjacent to Trading)
3. "Niche / Hobby" / "44"
4. "Community / Mastermind" / "43"
5. "Creator / Content" / "43"
6. "Fitness" / "35"
7. "SEO / Marketing" / "34"
8. "Side Hustle / Escape" / "33"
9. "Crypto / Web3" / "29"
10. "AI Tools" / "25"
11. "Coding / Dev" / "25"
12. "Ecommerce" / "20"
13. "Sports Betting" / "16"
14. "Agency / SMMA" / "16"
15. "Real Estate" / "10"
16. "Course / Education" / "9"
17. "Tech / Dev" / "6"
18. "Copywriting" / "5"
19. "Adult / OF Agency" / "4"
20. "Newsletter / Substack" / "2" (the smallest bubble)

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Niche name (inside each bubble): bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF, sized proportionally to bubble size (largest bubbles get larger text, smallest bubbles get smaller text)
- Store count (inside each bubble, beneath niche name): bold condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, sized proportionally

BUBBLE STYLING:
- Each bubble is a rounded rectangle with thin bright cyan #06B6D4 outline at low opacity.
- Bubble backgrounds: dark indigo with subtle inner gradient. The 3 largest bubbles (Trading, Self-Improvement, Niche / Hobby) have a slightly deeper purple-tinted fill to telegraph 'top tier of volume.' The bubbles for store counts 5 and below (Copywriting, Adult / OF Agency, Newsletter / Substack) have a slightly faded fill to telegraph 'long tail.'
- The Trading bubble (largest, count 59) has a brighter cyan glow ring around its outline as the visual anchor.
- Bubbles tile together cleanly with small consistent gaps. The treemap should feel packed, not scattered.

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces.
- No realistic icons inside the bubbles.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No real Whop store names or URLs anywhere.
- No real brand logos.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference visualization.

embedded-2.notes: Part 3 visual. The 6 Monetization Shapes Comparison Card. 6 columns x 5 rows.
embedded-2.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference table showing The 6 Monetization Shapes side-by-side. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels inside the image.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 6 Monetization Shapes"
SUBTITLE: "Price. Cadence. Format. LTV. Entry difficulty."

COMPOSITION: a 6-column comparison table with 5 data rows plus a header row of shape-name pills at the top. Each column represents one shape.

HEADER ROW (cyan pills above each column, uppercase):
Column 1: "PAID SIGNALS"
Column 2: "PAID COMMUNITY"
Column 3: "PAID COURSE"
Column 4: "PAID NEWSLETTER"
Column 5: "COACHING COHORT"
Column 6: "HYBRID"

ROW 1 (left label: "Price Range"):
Column 1: "$19-$250/mo"
Column 2: "$19-$97/mo"
Column 3: "$97-$997 once"
Column 4: "$19-$97/mo"
Column 5: "$497-$5,000"
Column 6: "$97-$497/mo"

ROW 2 (left label: "Cadence"):
Column 1: "Continuous"
Column 2: "Daily chat"
Column 3: "On-demand"
Column 4: "Weekly"
Column 5: "Weekly call"
Column 6: "Weekly + chat"

ROW 3 (left label: "Format"):
Column 1: "Alerts"
Column 2: "Discord access"
Column 3: "Video + PDF"
Column 4: "Email feed"
Column 5: "Cohort + chat"
Column 6: "Course + Discord"

ROW 4 (left label: "LTV Profile"):
Column 1: "Medium"
Column 2: "High"
Column 3: "Low"
Column 4: "Medium"
Column 5: "High"
Column 6: "Highest"

ROW 5 (left label: "Entry Difficulty"):
Column 1: "Medium"
Column 2: "Low"
Column 3: "Medium"
Column 4: "Low"
Column 5: "High"
Column 6: "High"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Header pills (top of each column): condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Left-side row labels: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF, on a small dark indigo pill background
- Cell values: medium sans-serif, off-white at 90%, centered in each cell
- Row 4 (LTV Profile) values are bold and slightly larger to telegraph the row most readers will scan first

CELL STYLING:
- Each cell is a rounded rectangle with thin bright cyan #06B6D4 outline at low opacity.
- Cell backgrounds: dark indigo with subtle inner gradient.
- Column 6 (Hybrid, the highest-LTV shape) has a brighter cyan glow ring around the entire column's outline, telegraphing it as the priority shape.
- Even spacing with comfortable gutters between cells.

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces.
- No realistic icons inside cells (no money symbols as graphic icons, no megaphones, no calendars-as-icons).
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No real Whop store names or URLs.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference table.

embedded-3.notes: Part 6 visual. Pricing Distribution chart. 4 zones with the share of stores at each.
embedded-3.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference chart showing the Pricing Distribution across the 512 Whop stores. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels inside the image.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "Pricing Distribution"
SUBTITLE: "4 zones. Where the 512 stores cluster."

COMPOSITION: 4 large vertical column-bar zones arranged horizontally across the frame, left to right. Each zone is a tall rounded-rectangle column. Column heights are proportional to the share of stores at that zone (taller column = more stores priced in that zone). Each column has a label block at the top and detail labels at the bottom.

COLUMN 1 (leftmost, medium height, about 22% of stores):
Top label pill: "$19 STARTER ZONE"
Main percentage inside the column (large, centered): "~22%"
Bottom detail line: "$9-$29/mo. Impulse buy. High churn."
Bottom example chip: "e.g. signals + free-tier funnels"

COLUMN 2 (left of center, the tallest column, about 41% of stores):
Top label pill: "$47-$97 COMMITMENT ZONE"
Main percentage inside the column (large, centered): "~41%"
Bottom detail line: "$39-$97/mo. The crowded middle."
Bottom example chip: "e.g. most communities + courses"

COLUMN 3 (right of center, second tallest, about 25% of stores):
Top label pill: "$197-$497 OUTCOME ZONE"
Main percentage inside the column (large, centered): "~25%"
Bottom detail line: "$197-$497. Premium tier."
Bottom example chip: "e.g. premium signals + cohorts"

COLUMN 4 (rightmost, shortest, about 12% of stores):
Top label pill: "$997+ PREMIUM ZONE"
Main percentage inside the column (large, centered): "~12%"
Bottom detail line: "$997+. Mastermind tier."
Bottom example chip: "e.g. capped-seat coaching"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Top label pills above each column: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Main percentage inside each column: massive bold condensed sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Bottom detail line: medium sans-serif, off-white at 80% opacity
- Bottom example chip: italic small sans-serif, off-white at 70% opacity, inside a small rounded chip

COLUMN STYLING:
- Each column is a tall rounded rectangle with thin bright cyan #06B6D4 outline at low opacity.
- Column backgrounds: dark indigo with subtle inner gradient that intensifies slightly toward the bottom of each column.
- Column 2 (the $47-$97 commitment zone, where most stores cluster) has a brighter cyan glow ring around its outline, telegraphing it as the dominant zone.
- Even horizontal spacing with comfortable gutters between columns. Column heights vary proportionally to the percentages above.

BELOW THE COLUMNS, A SMALL FOOTNOTE STRIP:
"Free-tier-as-funnel pattern runs alongside all four zones. Most successful operators offer a free Whop as the discovery surface."

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces.
- No realistic icons (no money symbols as graphics, no piggy banks, no dollar-sign clip art).
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No real Whop store names or URLs in the example chips. Keep example chips as generic category descriptions only.
- No real brand logos.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference chart.

embedded-4.notes: Part 5 visual. The 5-Step 'Copy a Winning Pattern' Framework flow.
embedded-4.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference flow showing the 5-Step 'Copy a Winning Pattern' Framework. Aspect 5:4.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels inside the image.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The Pattern-Copy Framework"
SUBTITLE: "5 steps to extract any successful Whop store's pattern and redeploy in your niche."

COMPOSITION: 5 vertically-stacked step blocks arranged top to bottom, connected by short thin bright cyan #06B6D4 vertical arrows. Each step block contains a numbered step pill on the left, the step name as the main label, a one-line description beneath, and a small example chip on the right showing what that step extracts for the worked example.

DOWN THE LEFT EDGE: a thin vertical bright cyan #06B6D4 rail with small circular markers at each step node.

STEP BLOCK 1 (top):
Step pill: "STEP 1"
Main label: "Identify the Promise"
Sub-text: "What is the one-line outcome the buyer is paying for?"
Example chip on the right: "Pass prop firm phase 1"

STEP BLOCK 2:
Step pill: "STEP 2"
Main label: "Identify the Format"
Sub-text: "Which of the 6 monetization shapes is this? Signals, community, course, newsletter, cohort, or hybrid?"
Example chip on the right: "Course + community"

STEP BLOCK 3:
Step pill: "STEP 3"
Main label: "Identify the Cadence"
Sub-text: "How often does the buyer get something? Daily? Weekly? On-demand?"
Example chip on the right: "On-demand + ongoing chat"

STEP BLOCK 4:
Step pill: "STEP 4"
Main label: "Identify the Price Anchor"
Sub-text: "What does the price compare against? A coach? A loss? An industry-standard course?"
Example chip on the right: "vs cost of failing prop firm"

STEP BLOCK 5 (bottom, the destination):
Step pill: "STEP 5"
Main label: "Identify the Trust Signal"
Sub-text: "What proves they can deliver? Track record, experience, volume, testimonials?"
Example chip on the right: "Internal-system framing"

ARROWS:
- Between each step block, a short thin bright cyan #06B6D4 vertical arrow with a soft glow at the arrowhead.
- The flow reads strictly top to bottom.

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Step pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4 on dark indigo pill background, uppercase
- Main labels: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Sub-text: italic medium sans-serif, off-white at 75% opacity
- Example chips: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside small off-white rounded chip, smaller size

BLOCK STYLING:
- Each step block is a rounded rectangle with thin bright cyan outline at low opacity.
- Block backgrounds: dark indigo with subtle inner gradient.
- The bottom block (Step 5, the trust signal) has a brighter cyan glow ring around its outline as the visual destination, telegraphing it as the load-bearing element of the framework.
- Even vertical spacing between blocks.

LEFT-EDGE RAIL:
- Thin vertical bright cyan #06B6D4 line on the far-left edge of the composition.
- Small circular bright cyan node markers at each step's vertical position, connecting the rail to each step block.
- The rail ends with a slightly larger glowing node at Step 5.

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient. Subtle paper grain.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces.
- No realistic icons (no magnifying glasses, no clipboards, no checkmarks-as-decorative-icons, no lightbulbs, no shields).
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No real Whop store names or URLs in the example chips beyond the generic worked-example references already listed.
- No real brand logos.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference flow.
